macro_segments.title: Non-Fit Foods and Drinks on Yom Kippur: Exemptions and Objective vs. Subjective Standards

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Mishna on Food Unfit for Eating and Drink Unfit for Drinking
Rava's Examples: Peppercorns and Ginger as Non-Standard Foods (Moist vs. Dry States)
Objection from Baraita: Proof that Peppers are Food via Orlah Laws
Gemara's Resolution: Moist vs. Dry—Foods Change Status Based on Preparation
Himalta (Spiced Compound) from Handuai: Edible Raw, Permits Uncooking by Non-Jews
Reed Leaves vs. Grape Leaves: Freshness Threshold for Edibility
Fish Brine and Vinegar: Standards for Drinkable Liquids
Rebbi's View: Vinegar Settles the Soul (Meshivas Hanefesh)
Rav Gidal's Retraction: Vinegar Permitted Bidi'eved but Not L'chatchila; Small Amount Only
Distinction Between Eating/Drinking Categories and Meshivas Hanefesh (Soul-Settling) Effect

macro_segments.title: Chinuch (Training) of Minors in Fasting: Age-Based Gradations and Constitutional Differences

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Young Children Not Afflicted on Yom Kippur but Trained One or Two Years Prior
Rav Chisda: Health vs. Constitution Distinction in Training Timeline
Rav Huna's Age Progression: Hours at 8–9, Full Day D'Rabbanan at 10–11, Biblical at 12 (Girls)
Rav Nachman's Opinion: One Year Later—9–10 for Hours, 11–12 D'Rabbanan, 13 Biblical (Boys)
Gemara's Reconciliation: Moist vs. Dry (Health) Explaining Both Opinions
Rabbi Yochanan's Dissent: No Full-Day Obligation D'Rabbanan, Only Hours Until Obligation
Braita of Rabba bar Shmuel: Chinuch Done Only During Year Before Bar/Bat Mitzvah
Rava bar Ulla's Resolution: Two Types of Chinuch—Starting vs. Full Amount Before Maturity
Tosafot Yeshanim: Chinuch as Positive Performance (Mitzva Aseh) vs. Refraining from Sin
Practical Considerations: Parents' Role, Gender Differences, and Enforcement Limits

macro_segments.title: Pikuach Nefesh (Life-Saving) and the Three Cardinal Sins: Exceptions to Yom Kippur Restrictions

micro_segments.title: Pregnant Woman Smelling Food: Feeding Her to Settle Her Mind (Meshivas Hanefesh)
Sick Person: Consultation with Doctors and Self-Reporting When No Experts Available
Baraita's Gradations: Fork to Sauce, Then Sauce Alone, Then Fat, Then Meat—Lesser Sins Preferred
Principle: Nothing Stands Before Pikuach Nefesh Except the Big Three Cardinal Sins
Avodah Zarah (Idolatry): Derived from 'Love God with All Your Soul' — Require Martyrdom
Rebbi Eliezer's Drasha: Why Both 'Soul' and 'Might' in Shema—Teaching Life Over Wealth
Beyond Technical Avodah Zarah: Betrayal of God and Renunciation of Judaism
Gilui Arayot (Forbidden Sexual Relations) and Shfichut Damim (Murder): Taught via Rape Case
Mutual Teaching: Woman Pursued by Rapist Permits Killing Attacker; Murder Attacker Permits Killing
Rava's Story: Man Asked to Kill—'Your Blood Is Not Redder; Give Up Your Life'

macro_segments.title: Pikuach Nefesh (Life-Saving) and the Three Cardinal Sins: Exceptions to Yom Kippur Restrictions (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rambam on Coercion: One Forced at Gunpoint Is Oness (Not Culpable) Except for Forbidden Sex
Reason for Sex Exception: Erection Requires Willingness—Cannot Be Forced by Threat Alone

macro_segments.title: Story of Two Pregnant Women on Yom Kippur: Virtue vs. Wickedness Inferred from Fetal Need

micro_segments.title: First Woman: Whispered to About Yom Kippur, Calmed Down, No Feeding Needed
Rebbi's Judgment: Fetus Was Righteous (Betarem Etzarcha Ba'beten)
Connection to Rabbi Yochanan: Rebbi's Mother Pregnant with Him, Born Righteous
Second Woman: Whispered to But Not Calmed; Required Feeding on Yom Kippur
Rebbi's Judgment: Fetus Was Wicked (Zoru Resha'im Me'Rechem)
Shabtai Utzar Piri: Identified as Fruit Monopolizer Born from Second Woman
Tension: Necessity of Feeding vs. Moral Judgment and Potential Hesitation
